assertion_results.text: Impact map or task descriptions explicitly flag at least 3 ambiguities from the feature description
assertion_results.evidence: The impact-map.md contains 5 explicitly labeled ambiguities under '## Ambiguities and Open Questions': (1) 'Search should be faster' -- no performance target, (2) 'Results should be more relevant' -- no relevance definition, (3) 'Some kind of filtering capability' -- no filter specification, (4) 'Should be fast enough' (NFR) -- no quantified NFR, (5) 'Don't break existing functionality' -- no regression baseline. Each includes specific clarification questions.

assertion_results.text: Plan acknowledges that 'Better UI' (non-MVP) cannot be planned without design mockups or frontend repository and excludes it from scope
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md '## Out of Scope' section states: '"Better UI" (non-MVP): This requirement is marked as non-MVP and cannot be planned without design mockups or access to a frontend repository. The target repository (trustify-backend) is a Rust backend service with no frontend code. This requirement is excluded from the plan entirely.'

assertion_results.text: Tasks still follow the task-description-template.md format despite ambiguous requirements
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 task files contain the required template sections: ## Repository, ## Target Branch, ## Description, ## Files to Modify (or Files to Create), ## Implementation Notes, ## Acceptance Criteria, ## Test Requirements, ## Dependencies. Optional sections (API Changes, Reuse Candidates, Verification Commands) are included only where applicable, following the template rule 'Omit sections that don't apply'.

assertion_results.text: Tasks document assumptions where they fill in missing details, labeled as assumptions pending clarification
assertion_results.evidence: 5 of 6 tasks contain explicit 'ASSUMPTION (pending clarification):' labels: Task 1 ('The performance issue is database query execution time'), Task 2 ('More relevant means implementing weighted full-text search scoring using PostgreSQL ts_rank'), Task 3 ('Filters will include entity type, severity, date range, and license'), Task 4 ('Each entity list endpoint gets filters relevant to its domain'), Task 6 ('A short TTL cache is appropriate for search results'). Task 5 has no assumptions because it is a testing task that validates the other tasks' implementations.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 tasks contain '## Repository' followed by the single value 'trustify-backend'. No task lists multiple repositories.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 tasks contain: '## Target Branch' (set to 'main'), '## Description' (with substantive content), '## Acceptance Criteria' (with checkbox items), '## Test Requirements' (with checkbox items). Verified in task-1-add-search-indexes.md, task-2-search-relevance-scoring.md, task-3-search-endpoint-filters.md, task-4-entity-list-filters.md, task-5-search-integration-tests.md, task-6-search-caching.md.

assertion_results.text: File paths in Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths from the repo-backend.md mock repository structure manifest, not invented paths
assertion_results.evidence: All file paths in Files to Modify match the repo-backend.md directory tree: migration/src/lib.rs, modules/search/src/service/mod.rs, modules/search/src/endpoints/mod.rs, common/src/db/query.rs, modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/list.rs, modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/list.rs, modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs, tests/api/search.rs. The one Files to Create entry (migration/src/m0002_search_indexes/mod.rs) follows the established naming pattern from the existing migration/src/m0001_initial/mod.rs in the manifest.

assertion_results.text: Implementation Notes in every task reference specific file paths and code patterns from the repository, not abstract or generic guidance
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1 references migration/src/m0001_initial/mod.rs, entity/src/sbom.rs, entity/src/advisory.rs, entity/src/package.rs. Task 2 references modules/search/src/service/mod.rs, common/src/db/query.rs, common/src/model/paginated.rs, common/src/error.rs. Task 3 references modules/search/src/endpoints/mod.rs, modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/summary.rs, modules/fundamental/src/package/model/summary.rs. Task 4 references common/src/db/query.rs, modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs, modules/fundamental/src/advisory/service/advisory.rs, modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs. Task 5 references tests/api/search.rs, tests/api/sbom.rs, tests/api/advisory.rs. Task 6 references modules/search/src/endpoints/mod.rs, server/src/main.rs. All tasks reference specific patterns like SeaORM migration traits, ts_rank, PaginatedResults<T>, AppError, assert_eq! patterns.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Target Branch section set to 'main'
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 tasks contain '## Target Branch' followed by 'main': task-1 line 5, task-2 line 5, task-3 line 5, task-4 line 5, task-5 line 5, task-6 line 5.

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a format-tagged SHA-256 hash -- exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' (or sha256-adf). The digest is computed by re-fetching the description from the API and running scripts/sha256-digest.py
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 digest files contain the correct format. digest-task-1.md: 'sha256-md:449ddce5381ea66fd8ef121cadd2a33ac265d647b12796e3b209ea3724c5213c' (64 hex chars). digest-task-2.md: 'sha256-md:cf9fd888dc98d48eef91aaccb6978cc11bc093149cbde1dc85eb9bb572d7ab9c'. digest-task-3.md: 'sha256-md:dfdf62377254aef6c29708c0555efa6f2700f9ee840e8e202c58825db9faa9af'. digest-task-4.md: 'sha256-md:d79251c819797216cd34921b71d4f1d6d9ec961566d5a6547792f59cbb79dd47'. digest-task-5.md: 'sha256-md:ea2b488b42d4867ffba253897913bb9442802494a76a8791a41ef3cb9c104906'. digest-task-6.md: 'sha256-md:7ef74da5636794399ce042976bf9deedf8f148a2a60f8e499a0297b48a195bc8'. All use '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:' prefix with exactly 64 lowercase hex characters.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention -- inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: All convention rationales use the prescribed format. Task 1: 'Applies: convention has no file-type restriction (broadly applicable).' (correct for unscoped conventions). Task 2: 'Applies: task modifies `modules/search/src/service/mod.rs` matching the convention's `.rs` file scope.' Task 3: 'Applies: task modifies `modules/search/src/endpoints/mod.rs` matching the convention's `.rs` file scope.' Task 4: 'Applies: task modifies `common/src/db/query.rs` matching the convention's `.rs` file scope.' Task 5: 'Applies: task modifies `tests/api/search.rs` matching the convention's `.rs` file scope.' Task 6: 'Applies: task modifies `modules/search/src/endpoints/mod.rs` matching the convention's `.rs` file scope.' No inapplicable conventions are listed with 'Not applicable' annotations. No free-form prose rationales are present.
